results.possibleIdentity: Sheldon Gersten (born July 23, 1946) is a computer programmer and technical professional who was active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was a graduate student at New York University (NYU) and was affiliated with Mount Sinai Hospital in New York City. His technical expertise includes mathematics, programming, and digital systems. Gersten was a registered participant in the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) Technical Services Program, an initiative that facilitated collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. During his career, he was a key programmer for the development of APLUM, a version of the APL (A Programming Language) system designed for the CDC 6600 and CYBER computers at the University of Massachusetts at Amherst. His work on the APLUM project specifically focused on mixed functions and shared variables. He is originally from Brooklyn, New York.
